decisions.title: Auth module shipped with zero P0 bugs
decisions.detail: Alex confirmed that the authentication module was shipped last Friday with no P0 bugs.

evidence.quote: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as was agreed in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails are not being rate-limited
decisions.detail: Carol flagged a current risk that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Maximum 3 password reset attempts per hour
decisions.detail: A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon in the security review on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.

decisions.title: API Gateway to use AWS API Gateway with 100 req/min limit per user
decisions.detail: The API gateway will utilize AWS API Gateway, implementing a limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on Ben's recommendation from load tests.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 req/min limit per user.

decisions.title: Mobile app team blocked until Auth API spec is published
decisions.detail: The mobile app team is currently blocked and cannot proceed until the authentication API specification is published.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Target publish date for Auth API spec is 2026-07-05
decisions.detail: The target publish date for the Auth API specification is 2026-07-05.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests for auth endpoints required before Sprint 3 sign-off
decisions.detail: Carol requires all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests completed before Sprint 3 sign-off.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: Fallback behaviour if API gateway goes down
decisions.detail: An open question exists regarding the fallback behaviour if the API gateway experiences downtime.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Refresh token invalidation on password reset
decisions.detail: An open question remains whether refresh tokens are invalidated automatically when a password is reset.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement rate limiting for password reset functionality, ensuring a maximum of 3 attempts per hour.
Develop and implement automated integration tests for the login, refresh token, and password reset authentication endpoints.
Finalize and publish the Authentication API specification.

constraints.text: Maximum 3 password reset attempts permitted per hour.
API Gateway rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user must be enforced.
Authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05 to unblock the mobile app team.
Automated integration tests for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) are required before Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours.
API Gateway will utilize AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the required fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway experiences downtime?
Should refresh tokens be invalidated automatically when a user's password is reset?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user successfully obtains a JWT token, when 24 hours have elapsed since the token was issued, then subsequent API requests using that token must be rejected as unauthorized.
Given a user successfully obtains a JWT token, when less than 24 hours have elapsed since the token was issued, then subsequent API requests using that token must be successfully authorized.

Given a user requests a password reset for a specific email address, when 3 password reset attempts have already been made for that email within the last hour, then no further password reset email is sent for that email within the current hour, and the system provides an appropriate user message indicating the limit has been reached.

Given a user requests a password reset for a specific email address, when fewer than 3 password reset attempts have been made for that email within the last hour, then a password reset email is successfully sent to the user.
Given an authenticated user, when the user sends 100 API requests within a 60-second period, then all 100 requests are successfully processed.
Given an authenticated user, when the user sends 101 API requests within a 60-second period, then the 101st request is rejected with an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests status code.
test_scenarios: Successfully log in, receive a JWT token, and verify it works immediately. Wait 24 hours and 1 minute, then try to use the same token and verify authentication fails.
Successfully log in, receive a JWT token, and verify it works immediately. Wait 23 hours and 59 minutes, then try to use the same token and verify authentication still works.
Initiate password reset 3 times for the same email address within a 5-minute window and verify that the system indicates 3 attempts were successful (e.g., 3 emails sent or 3 success messages).
Initiate password reset a 4th time for the same email address within the same 5-minute window and verify no email is sent and a rate limit error/message is displayed to the user.
Initiate password reset for an email, wait 61 minutes, then initiate again and verify an email is successfully sent.
Send exactly 100 API requests as a single authenticated user within 50 seconds and confirm all requests return successful responses.
Send 101 API requests as a single authenticated user within 50 seconds and confirm the 101st request returns an HTTP 429 status code.
Send 100 API requests as User A and 100 API requests as User B concurrently within 50 seconds, verifying all 200 requests succeed without rate limiting User A or User B.
edge_cases: For password reset rate limiting: What if the email address provided for password reset does not exist? Does it count towards the 3 attempts per hour limit for that non-existent email to prevent enumeration?
For password reset rate limiting: What if multiple password reset attempts are made concurrently for the same user?
For API Gateway rate limiting: What specific mechanism identifies a 'user' for the 100 requests per minute limit? Is it based on an authenticated session ID, IP address, or something else?
unresolved_behaviors: When a user's password is reset, should their existing refresh tokens be automatically invalidated?

decision_history: Alex confirmed the authentication module was shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.
The JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as was agreed in Sprint 2.
A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon in the security review on 2026-06-28.
The API gateway will utilize AWS API Gateway, implementing a limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on Ben's recommendation from load tests.
Automated integration tests for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) are required before Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: The mobile app team is blocked and cannot proceed until the authentication API specification is published.
Blocked by: Definition of fallback behaviour if the API gateway experiences downtime (owned by System Analyst).
risks: Password reset emails are currently not being rate-limited (Likelihood: Confirmed, Impact: High - security vulnerability).
open_questions: Are refresh tokens invalidated automatically when a password is reset? (Impact: High, clarifies a security requirement, owned by PM/QA).
milestones: Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset endpoints completed before Sprint 3 sign-off.

system_requirements: The system SHALL configure issued JWT tokens to expire after 24 hours.
The system SHALL implement rate limiting for password reset request submissions (inferred — needs confirmation).
The system SHALL limit a user to a maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour.
The authentication module SHALL include automated integration tests for its login, refresh, and reset endpoints prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.
data_flows: User authentication requests flow from client applications (e.g., mobile app) to the Authentication Module via the AWS API Gateway.
JWT tokens are issued by the Authentication Module and returned to client applications via the AWS API Gateway.
Password reset requests flow from client applications to the Authentication Module via the AWS API Gateway.
Password reset emails are sent by the Authentication Module (or an integrated email service) to user email addresses.
interface_contracts: Authentication API Contract: Defines endpoints for user login, token refresh, and password reset. This contract includes request/response schemas, error codes, and authentication mechanisms (e.g., JWT).
API Gateway Configuration Contract: Specifies a request throttling policy of 100 requests per minute per user.

constraints.text: The API gateway MUST enforce a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.
The authentication API specification MUST be published to unblock the mobile app team.

architectural_decisions.text: The system's API Gateway functionality SHALL be implemented using AWS API Gateway.
The authentication mechanism SHALL use JWT tokens with a 24-hour expiry.

open_analysis_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior and system response if the AWS API Gateway experiences downtime or becomes unreachable?
SHALL refresh tokens be automatically invalidated when a user's password is reset? If so, what is the mechanism for invalidation?

stakeholder.business_summary: The foundational user authentication system has been delivered successfully with no critical issues. However, our mobile app development is currently stalled because the full technical specifications for the authentication system are not yet published, although a target date of July 5th has been set. There's also a notable security concern regarding unrestricted password reset emails, which needs immediate attention.
key_impact: The core user login experience is stable and delivered to a high quality standard.
Mobile app development timelines are directly impacted and delayed until the authentication details are finalized and shared.
There is a significant security vulnerability related to password resets that could be exploited if not addressed.
User security might be compromised if old login sessions remain active after a password reset.
major_unresolved_questions: How will we prevent a security risk where an unlimited number of password reset emails can be requested, despite an agreement for a maximum of 3 attempts per hour?
What is the plan if the system that handles all incoming requests (API gateway) experiences an outage, to ensure business continuity?
When a user resets their password, will all their active login sessions be automatically ended to enhance security?

issues.description: The PM View states that the definition of fallback behaviour for API Gateway downtime is 'owned by System Analyst'. Decision D009 only states this is an open question, without assigning ownership.
issues.suggestion: Remove the attribution of ownership ('owned by System Analyst') or indicate that this ownership is an inferred assignment needing confirmation.

issues.description: The PM View states that the question regarding refresh token invalidation on password reset is 'owned by PM/QA'. Decision D010 only states this is an open question, without assigning ownership.
issues.suggestion: Remove the attribution of ownership ('owned by PM/QA') or indicate that this ownership is an inferred assignment needing confirmation.

issues.description: The PM View adds 'Impact: High, clarifies a security requirement' to the open question D010. While this might be a reasonable inference, it's an interpretation of the decision's significance rather than a direct quote or fact from the extracted decision.
issues.suggestion: Rephrase to clarify it's an inferred impact/significance, or remove the added detail if it's not directly supported by the source.

issues.description: The Stakeholder View states 'User security might be compromised if old login sessions remain active after a password reset.' This presents a potential negative outcome as an impact related to an open question (D010). D010 is an open question about *if* refresh tokens are invalidated, not a confirmed risk or impact.
issues.suggestion: Rephrase to frame this as an unresolved question that *could* lead to a security impact if not addressed, rather than stating it as a potential compromise, to align with the 'open question' nature of D010.

review.reviewer_notes: The Engineer, QA, and SA views are accurate and well-supported by the extracted decisions. The PM and Stakeholder views introduce unsupported ownership claims and overstate the certainty of potential impacts from open questions. These issues need to be addressed before publishing.
